item.title: [9] X-ray linear dichroism in the kagome antiferromagnet Mn3Sn
item.description:
Last authors: Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

Authors: Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: High relevance to kagome magnetism; experimental spectroscopy on key material.

Tags: #kagome #ARPES #magnetic

Abstract: Author(s): Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, and Shinji Miwa We report the observation of x-ray linear dichroism (XLD) at the Mn ${L}_{2,3}$ edges in an epitaxial W/${\mathrm{Mn}}_{3}\mathrm{Sn}$ thin film with kagome planes oriented out of plane. A finite XLD signal is observed between linear polarizations parallel and perpendicular to the kagome planes, ref… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174418] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

Authors: Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: High relevance to kagome magnetism; experimental spectroscopy on key material.

Tags: #kagome #ARPES #magnetic

Abstract: Author(s): Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, and Shinji Miwa We report the observation of x-ray linear dichroism (XLD) at the Mn ${L}_{2,3}$ edges in an epitaxial W/${\mathrm{Mn}}_{3}\mathrm{Sn}$ thin film with kagome planes oriented out of plane. A finite XLD signal is observed between linear polarizations parallel and perpendicular to the kagome planes, ref… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174418] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] X-ray linear dichroism in the kagome antiferromagnet Mn3Sn
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa; Authors: Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Observation of Bethe strings in the quantum spin chain antiferromagnet BaCo2V2O8
item.description:
Last authors: Jianda Wu, Bella Lake

Authors: Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Excellent experimental neutron scattering study on quantum spin chains.

Tags: #neutronscattering

Abstract: Author(s): Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, Jiahao Yang, Paul Steffens, Martin Boehm, Jianda Wu, and Bella Lake Bethe strings, complex bound states of magnetic excitations, are a fundamental yet long-sought exotic states of 1D quantum Heisenberg magnets. Various string states are observed via inelastic neutron scattering measurements in the spin-chain compound BaCo 2 V 2 O 8 in an applied longitudinal magnetic field. The experimental data exhibit quantitatively excellent agreement with theoretical predictions across a wide field range, providing unambiguous validation of Bethe string excitations. These results reveal the underlying spectrum, including at high energies, of spin chains in the quantum critical region. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174419] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Jianda Wu, Bella Lake

Authors: Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Excellent experimental neutron scattering study on quantum spin chains.

Tags: #neutronscattering

Abstract: Author(s): Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, Jiahao Yang, Paul Steffens, Martin Boehm, Jianda Wu, and Bella Lake Bethe strings, complex bound states of magnetic excitations, are a fundamental yet long-sought exotic states of 1D quantum Heisenberg magnets. Various string states are observed via inelastic neutron scattering measurements in the spin-chain compound BaCo 2 V 2 O 8 in an applied longitudinal magnetic field. The experimental data exhibit quantitatively excellent agreement with theoretical predictions across a wide field range, providing unambiguous validation of Bethe string excitations. These results reveal the underlying spectrum, including at high energies, of spin chains in the quantum critical region. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174419] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Anisotropic metamagnetism and magnetotransport of heavy rare-earth orthorhombic single-crystal TbAlGe
item.description:
Last authors: S. R. Saha, J. Paglione

Authors: Ram Kumar, K. E. Avers, V. Saini, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Important transport study on a topological magnetic material.

Tags: #topological #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Ram Kumar, K. E. Avers, V. Saini, D. S. Sokratov, Y. Anand, P. Saraf, J. A. Horn, N. Brenowitz, S. Otazo, P. Sobel, D. Graf, S. R. Saha, and J. Paglione We report a comprehensive investigation of the anisotropic magnetism and magnetic field-induced transitions in single crystals of the orthorhombic system TbAlGe, a member of the topological $R\mathrm{AlGe}$ (where $R$ denotes rare earth) family with the highest ordering temeprature in the $R\mathrm{… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174423] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Last authors: S. R. Saha, J. Paglione

item.title: [9] Controlling correlations of a polaritonic Luttinger liquid by engineered cross-Kerr nonlinearity
item.description:
Last authors: Anushree Dey, Bimalendu Deb

Authors: Nabaneet Sharma, Anushree Dey, Bimalendu Deb

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: Luttinger liquid

Tags: #Luttinger #superconduct

Abstract: Author(s): Nabaneet Sharma, Anushree Dey, and Bimalendu Deb We study correlation properties of polaritons at zero temperature in a multiconnected Jaynes-Cummings lattice on a superconducting circuit quantum electrodynamics platform with engineered cross-Kerr nonlinearity that mimics attractive nearest-neighbor interaction. A multiconnected Jaynes-Cummings la… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174520] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [8] Nonvolatile control of nonlinear Hall and circular photogalvanic effects via Berry curvature dipole in multiferroic monolayer CrNBr2
item.description:
Last authors: Guibo Zheng, Fangping Ouyang

Authors: Wenzhe Zhou, Yinheng Li, Dehe Zhang, Guibo Zheng, Fangping Ouyang

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Strong interest in Berry curvature and topological phenomena in 2D materials.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #monolayer #Halleffect

Abstract: Author(s): Wenzhe Zhou, Yinheng Li, Dehe Zhang, Guibo Zheng, and Fangping Ouyang The Berry curvature dipole induced by symmetry breaking plays a pivotal role in electronic transport properties and nonlinear responses, such as the nonlinear Hall effect and circular photogalvanic effect. The study of the Berry curvature dipole is often explored in time-reversal symmetric systems, … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195126] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Last authors: Guibo Zheng, Fangping Ouyang

Authors: Wenzhe Zhou, Yinheng Li, Dehe Zhang, Guibo Zheng, Fangping Ouyang

The study of the Berry curvature dipole is often explored in time-reversal symmetric systems, … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195126] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Abelian and non-Abelian fractionalized states in twisted MoTe2: A generalized Landau-level theory
item.description:
Last authors: Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Authors: Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of fractional Chern insulators in moire systems relevant to the user's broader interest area.

Tags: #moiré #fractionalCherninsulator

Abstract: Author(s): Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, and Fengcheng Wu Here, the authors develop a framework that variationally decomposes Bloch bands into generalized Landau levels (LLs), enabling a quantitative characterization of their effective LL nature. Applied to twisted bilayer MoTe 2 , the first band is dominated by the generalized zeroth LL, supporting Abelian fractional Chern insulators. The second band, at specific twist angles, is dominated by the generalized first LL and exhibits numerical evidence consistent with a non-Abelian Moore–Read state. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195129] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Authors: Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of fractional Chern insulators in moire systems relevant to the user's broader interest area.

Tags: #moiré #fractionalCherninsulator

Abstract: Author(s): Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, and Fengcheng Wu Here, the authors develop a framework that variationally decomposes Bloch bands into generalized Landau levels (LLs), enabling a quantitative characterization of their effective LL nature. Applied to twisted bilayer MoTe 2 , the first band is dominated by the generalized zeroth LL, supporting Abelian fractional Chern insulators. The second band, at specific twist angles, is dominated by the generalized first LL and exhibits numerical evidence consistent with a non-Abelian Moore–Read state. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195129] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Abelian and non-Abelian fractionalized states in twisted MoTe2: A generalized Landau-level theory

item.title: [9] Pressure-modulated competition between dual charge density waves in NbSe3
item.description:
Last authors: Wenge Yang, Moritz Hoesch

Authors: Yongsheng Zhao, Konstantin Glazyrin, Pablo J. Bereciartua, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct experimental study on NbSe3, a quasi-1D material explicitly mentioned in the user profile.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #CDW #NbSe3

Abstract: Author(s): Yongsheng Zhao, Konstantin Glazyrin, Pablo J. Bereciartua, Christian Plueckthun, Satishkumar Kulkarni, Nana Li, Limin Yan, Kai Zhang, Bihan Wang, Yiming Wang, Jianbo Zhang, Nico Giordano, Helmuth Berger, Xin Wang, Sonia Francoual, Wenge Yang, and Moritz Hoesch Controlling collective electronic states through clean and reversible external stimuli is crucial for realizing functional quantum materials. Here, we report a study on temperature ($T$) and pressure ($P$)-tuned charge-density-wave (CDW) orders in the quasi-one-dimensional system of $\mathrm{NbS}{\m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195130] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Pressure-driven metal-insulator transition in BaMn2P2: A correlated Mn-pnictide analog of Fe-based superconductors

Last authors: Timur Kim, Henrik M. Rønnow

Authors: Youngro Lee, Daniil Evtushynsky, Richard Gaal, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant correlated electron system study in the context of Fe-based superconductivity interest.

Tags: #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Youngro Lee, Daniil Evtushynsky, Richard Gaal, Igor Morozov, Anita Maksutova, Tomasz Poreba, Timur Kim, and Henrik M. Rønnow We report pressure-induced metallization in ${\mathrm{BaMn}}_{2}{\mathrm{P}}_{2}$ based on resistivity measurements in a diamond anvil cell. At ambient pressure, the temperature-dependent resistivity is well described by a two-gap Arrhenius model, yielding intrinsic and extrinsic activation energies… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195131] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Tags: #quasi-one-dimensional #Kondo

Abstract: Author(s): Chao Mu, Jiabin Song, Le Wang, Youguo Shi, Yi-feng Yang, Zheng Li, and Jianlin Luo We report $^{59}\mathrm{Co}$ and $^{71}\mathrm{Ga}$ nuclear magnetic resonance investigations of the quasi-one-dimensional Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{8}$. Below the coherence temperature, ${T}^{*}≈\phantom{\rule{4pt}{0ex}}29\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$, the … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195134] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Elliptical orbit motion of moiré exciton wave packets in twisted van der Waals heterobilayers
item.description:
Last authors: F. M. Peeters, D. R. da Costa

Authors: J. Rubstein, Andrey Chaves, T. A. S. Pereira, R. N. Costa Filho, F. M. Peeters, D. R. da Costa

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant theoretical work on exciton dynamics in twisted moire systems.

Tags: #moiré #vanderWaals

Abstract: Author(s): J. Rubstein, Andrey Chaves, T. A. S. Pereira, R. N. Costa Filho, F. M. Peeters, and D. R. da Costa Twisted heterobilayers of transition metal dichalcogenides exhibit a moiré pattern that translates into a honeycomb lattice of single-particle gap minima, thus resulting in a superlattice potential for excitons. The quasiparticle associated with the Bloch state of such excitons is known as a moiré e… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195428] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Last authors: F. M. Peeters, D. R. da Costa

Authors: J. Rubstein, Andrey Chaves, T. A. S. Pereira, R. N. Costa Filho, F. M. Peeters, D. R. da Costa

Why: Relevant theoretical work on exciton dynamics in twisted moire systems.

Tags: #moiré #vanderWaals

Abstract: Author(s): J. Rubstein, Andrey Chaves, T. A. S. Pereira, R. N. Costa Filho, F. M. Peeters, and D. R. da Costa Twisted heterobilayers of transition metal dichalcogenides exhibit a moiré pattern that translates into a honeycomb lattice of single-particle gap minima, thus resulting in a superlattice potential for excitons. The quasiparticle associated with the Bloch state of such excitons is known as a moiré e… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195428] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [8] Current-induced magneto-optical Kerr effect as a probe of Dirac carriers in Bi1−xSbx alloy
item.description:
Last authors: Masashi Kawaguchi, Masamitsu Hayashi

Authors: Ryota Miyazaki, Shunzhen Wang, Guanxiong Qu, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of Dirac semimetal alloy properties using optical probes.

Tags: #Dirac

Abstract: Author(s): Ryota Miyazaki, Shunzhen Wang, Guanxiong Qu, Yukihiro Marui, Yuta Kobayashi, Masashi Kawaguchi, and Masamitsu Hayashi We study the current-induced magneto-optical Kerr effect (MOKE) in ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{x}$ semimetalic alloys. The MOKE signal is found to be the largest in pure Bi ($x=0$), exceeding that of transition metals by nearly four orders of magnitude, and decreases monotonically with increa… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180406] Published Mon May 18, 2026

The MOKE signal is found to be the largest in pure Bi ($x=0$), exceeding that of transition metals by nearly four orders of magnitude, and decreases monotonically with increa… [Phys. Rev. item.title: [7] Floquet second-order topological insulator in strained graphene
item.description:
Last authors: Rui Wang, Dong-Hui Xu

Authors: Yu-Wen Xu, Xiaolin Wan, Zi-Ming Wang, Rui Wang, Dong-Hui Xu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Floquet band engineering in graphene is relevant to the user's broader interests.

Tags: #Dirac #topologicalinsulator #graphene

Abstract: Author(s): Yu-Wen Xu, Xiaolin Wan, Zi-Ming Wang, Rui Wang, and Dong-Hui Xu Graphene provides a canonical setting for Floquet band engineering, where circularly polarized light can dynamically open topological gaps at Dirac points and generate nonequilibrium Hall responses. Here we show that uniaxial strain and off-resonant circularly polarized light with tunable incidence … [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201113] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Here we show that uniaxial strain and off-resonant circularly polarized light with tunable incidence … [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201113] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [9] 3Q magnetic order with spatially alternating spin scalar chirality in overdoped Co0.336TaS2
item.description:
Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park

Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Excellent experimental study of complex spin texture and Hall physics in a topological-relevant material.

Tags: #topological #Halleffect #neutronscattering

Abstract: Author(s): Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, Yeochan An, Kazuki Iida, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Sakib Matin, Romain Sibille, Scott A. Crooker, and Je-Geun Park While underdoped Co 1 / 3 TaS 2 shows a topological Hall effect, the overdoped Co 0 . 336 TaS 2 variant does not. Traditionally, this was attributed to a simple helical spin order. However, using multimodal techniques—including inelastic neutron scattering and optical dichroism—the authors propose here a more complex triple-Q order. In this state, the spin scalar chirality alternates spatially, effectively cancelling the macroscopic Hall response. This study provides a new framework for detecting “hidden” multi-Q spin textures that standard transport measurements might miss. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174410] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park

Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Excellent experimental study of complex spin texture and Hall physics in a topological-relevant material.

Tags: #topological #Halleffect #neutronscattering

Abstract: Author(s): Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, Yeochan An, Kazuki Iida, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Sakib Matin, Romain Sibille, Scott A. Crooker, and Je-Geun Park While underdoped Co 1 / 3 TaS 2 shows a topological Hall effect, the overdoped Co 0 . 336 TaS 2 variant does not. Traditionally, this was attributed to a simple helical spin order. However, using multimodal techniques—including inelastic neutron scattering and optical dichroism—the authors propose here a more complex triple-Q order. In this state, the spin scalar chirality alternates spatially, effectively cancelling the macroscopic Hall response. This study provides a new framework for detecting “hidden” multi-Q spin textures that standard transport measurements might miss. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174410] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] 3Q magnetic order with spatially alternating spin scalar chirality in overdoped Co0.336TaS2
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Scott A. Crooker, Je-Geun Park; Authors: Woonghee Cho, Pyeongjae Park, Chaebin Kim, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Multidimensional control of altermagnetism via symmetry engineering in van der Waals heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Ke-Qiu Chen, Li-Ming Tang

Authors: Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of altermagnetism in vdW heterostructures, relevant to spintronics and magnetic topology.

Tags: #altermagnet #vanderWaals #heterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Xin Chen, Jingbiao Yuan, Haiyu Meng, Shibo Fang, Yee Sin Ang, Xiong-Xiong Xue, Ke-Qiu Chen, and Li-Ming Tang Altermagnets enable nonrelativistic spin splitting without net magnetization, benefiting miniaturized and highly integrated spintronic devices. In this work, we investigate the physical mechanism by which ferroelectric substrates induce altermagnetism in antiferromagnetic materials through symmetry … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174411] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [8] Dependence of superconducting and pseudogap quasiparticle dynamics on the number of CuO2 planes in Bi-based cuprate superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Hiroshi Eisaki, Migaku Oda

Authors: Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant study of unconventional superconductors, fits broad interest profile.

Tags: #cuprates #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Taisei Shimizu, Tohru Kurosawa, Satoshi Tsuchiya, Yasunori Toda, Shigeyuki Ishida, Hiroshi Eisaki, and Migaku Oda The maximum superconducting transition temperature ${T}_{c,\mathrm{max}}$ in cuprate superconductors exhibits a strong dependence on the number of ${\mathrm{CuO}}_{2}$ planes $n$ per structural cell, exceeding 100 K in trilayer compounds ($n=3$). Elucidating the mechanism responsible for this enhanc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174510] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Hiroshi Eisaki, Migaku Oda

Elucidating the mechanism responsible for this enhanc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174510] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [7] Theory of reentrant superconductivity in Corbino Josephson junctions
item.description:
Last authors: Philip Kim, Yuval Oreg

Authors: Omri Lesser, Joon Young Park, Yuval Ronen, Thomas Werkmeister, Philip Kim, Yuval Oreg

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of topological insulator/superconductor junctions, directly relevant.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Omri Lesser, Joon Young Park, Yuval Ronen, Thomas Werkmeister, Philip Kim, and Yuval Oreg Josephson junctions made of conventional superconductors display Fraunhofer-like oscillations of the critical current as a function of the threaded magnetic flux. When the superconductors are deposited on the surface of a three-dimensional topological insulator, this pattern is slightly modified due… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174511] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [8] Evolution of superconductivity evidence in pressurized La3−xSmxNi2O7
item.description:
Last authors: Hualei Sun, Meng Wang

Authors: Qingyi Zhong, Junfeng Chen, Zhengyang Qiu, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Follow-up on high-Tc nickelates, highly relevant for superconducting material interests.

Tags: #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Qingyi Zhong, Junfeng Chen, Zhengyang Qiu, Jingyuan Li, Xing Huang, Peiyue Ma, Mengwu Huo, Hongliang Dong, Sihao Deng, Lunhua He, Yifeng Han, Hualei Sun, and Meng Wang Motivated by the discovery of superconductivity in bilayer ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ at 80 K and the increased superconducting transition temperature, ${T}_{\text{c}}$, up to 96 K in single crystals of ${\mathrm{La}}_{1.57}{\mathrm{Sm}}_{1.43}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174512] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [7] Inherent momentum-dependent gap structure of altermagnetic superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Andreas Kreisel, Brian M. Andersen

Authors: Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory connecting altermagnetism to unconventional superconducting gap symmetry.

Tags: #altermagnet #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, Mercè Roig, Daniel F. Agterberg, Andreas Kreisel, and Brian M. Andersen The authors study here superconductivity in altermagnetic metals using microscopic models, investigating the importance of the sublattice degree of freedom. They demonstrate that the sublattice polarization of the altermagnetic state imprints an anisotropic structure on the superconducting gap with nodes at the Brillouin zone boundary, despite arising from a momentum-independent bare interaction. In the limit of large spin-split bands, nonunitary equal-spin triplet superconductivity is favored by longer-range same-sublattice pairing. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174513] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Andreas Kreisel, Brian M. Andersen

Authors: Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory connecting altermagnetism to unconventional superconducting gap symmetry.

Tags: #altermagnet #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Christian L. H. Rasmussen, Jannik Gondolf, Mats Barkman, Mercè Roig, Daniel F. Agterberg, Andreas Kreisel, and Brian M. Andersen The authors study here superconductivity in altermagnetic metals using microscopic models, investigating the importance of the sublattice degree of freedom. They demonstrate that the sublattice polarization of the altermagnetic state imprints an anisotropic structure on the superconducting gap with nodes at the Brillouin zone boundary, despite arising from a momentum-independent bare interaction. In the limit of large spin-split bands, nonunitary equal-spin triplet superconductivity is favored by longer-range same-sublattice pairing. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174513] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Inherent momentum-dependent gap structure of altermagnetic superconductors

item.title: [7] Strange metal and Fermi arcs from disordering spin stripes
item.description:
Last authors: Xu Zhang, Nick Bultinck

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Relevant to nematicity and cuprate-like physics.

Tags: #nematic #Fermisurface

Abstract: Author(s): Xu Zhang and Nick Bultinck We revisit the effective theory for fluctuating spin stripes coupled to a Fermi surface and consider the parameter regime where a spin-nematic phase intervenes between the spin-density-wave state and the symmetric state. It is shown that adding potential disorder to this theory, which acts as an unc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195112] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Xu Zhang, Nick Bultinck

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Tags: #nematic #Fermisurface

It is shown that adding potential disorder to this theory, which acts as an unc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195112] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [9] Pressure-induced electronic band evolution and observation of superconductivity in the Dirac semimetal ZrTe5
item.description:
Last authors: Jinguang Cheng, Prashant Shahi

Authors: Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct interest: electronic structure and SC in a topological Dirac semimetal.

Tags: #Dirac #superconductivity #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, Rajan Walia, Jianping Sun, Genfu Chen, Dilip Bhoi, Sandip Chatterjee, Yoshiya Uwatoko, Jinguang Cheng, and Prashant Shahi We report a comprehensive investigation of the pressure effects on the magnetotransport properties of the topological material ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{5}$ within 1–8 GPa pressure range. With increasing pressure, the characteristic peak (${T}_{p}$) in its electrical resistivity $ρ(T)$ first shifts to highe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195113] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Jinguang Cheng, Prashant Shahi

Authors: Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct interest: electronic structure and SC in a topological Dirac semimetal.

Tags: #Dirac #superconductivity #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Sanskar Mishra, Nagendra Singh, Vinod K. Gangwar, Rajan Walia, Jianping Sun, Genfu Chen, Dilip Bhoi, Sandip Chatterjee, Yoshiya Uwatoko, Jinguang Cheng, and Prashant Shahi We report a comprehensive investigation of the pressure effects on the magnetotransport properties of the topological material ${\mathrm{ZrTe}}_{5}$ within 1–8 GPa pressure range. With increasing pressure, the characteristic peak (${T}_{p}$) in its electrical resistivity $ρ(T)$ first shifts to highe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195113] Published Thu May 14, 2026
